arts.ttl: Brighton player ratings vs Leicester: Evan Ferguson struggles and a calamitous 4/10
Jamie Vardy inspires late comeback as Leicester draw with Brighton
arts.athr: Peter Lansley at the King Power Stadium
arts.sum: Where there’s Jamie Vardy, there’s hope. Ruud van Nistelrooy has joked with reporters to stop pointing out that the Leicester City legend stole his record for scoring in 11 consecutive Premier League games in 2015, but right now he is delighted the veteran striker is providing a goalscoring edge at the start of a new era at the King Power Stadium. As Brighton coasted toward the 2-0 victory that would have sent them back into the Champions League places, with 86 minutes on the clock, it was Vardy who volleyed home to inspire hope of a comeback that had never looked on as Brighton created chance after chance, just as West Ham United had done on Tuesday night here. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Leicester City next five Premier League fixtures compared to Ipswich, Wolves and Southampton
arts.sum: With that being said, we take a look at the Foxes' next five fixtures and how they compare to their relegation rivals across December and the Christmas period. Leicester's next five Premier League fixtures

arts.ttl: Ruud's Leicester rally to steal point vs Brighton
arts.sum: Highlights of the Premier League match between Leicester City and Brighton & Hove Albion.

arts.ttl: Leicester City player ratings v Brighton as Jamie Vardy sparks improbable comeback - Jordan Blackwell - Leicestershire Live
arts.sum: Jamie Vardy got the first, coolly finishing after pouncing on a deflection, but it was substitute Bobby De Cordova-Reid who delivered the equaliser, the Jamaican tapping in his first City goal as the clock hit 90. Mads Hermansen needed to produce an excellent save to keep out Pervis Estupinan’s fierce drive to the top corner, and then Kaoru Mitoma blazed over when well placed after City gave up possession in their own half.

arts.ttl: Leicester City 2-2 Brighton & Hove Albion: Highlights, man of the match, stats as Jamie Vardy stars in dramatic draw
arts.sum: Jamie Vardy goal and assist inspires Leicester City to stage a late comeback to draw 2-2 with Brighton & Hove Albion in the Premier League on Sunday afternoon.

arts.ttl: Leicester stage dramatic comeback to rescue point against Brighton
arts.sum: Jamie Vardy and Bobby De Cordova-Reid hit late goals.

arts.ttl: Leicester transfer news as Man Utd deal chased but one thing must happen first
arts.sum: Ruud van Nistelrooy wants to bring Toby Collyer to Leicester City as he looks for a favour from Manchester United. The Dutchman is eager to bolster his squad when the January transfer window opens next month and has reportedly targeted a move for United youngster Collyer.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace boss in damning verdict on Leicester City situation and says 'it makes no sense'
arts.sum: Crystal Palace boss Oliver Glasner has issued a withering verdict on Odsonne Edouard's loan at Leicester City and insisted recalling the striker "makes no sense". From our side, It makes no sense to bring him back because if he’s not playing at Leicester, then why should he play for Crystal Palace?

arts.ttl: Leicester City team news v Brighton as Ruud van Nistelrooy forced into change
arts.sum: City (4-2-3-1): Hermansen; Justin, Coady, Vestergaard, Kristiansen; Ndidi, Soumare; McAteer, Ayew, El Khannouss; Vardy. It means Conor Coady and Jannik Vestergaard again for the City centre-back pairing, with Wout Faes left out for the second game running.
